suppress.notes: False positive: spring-boot-netty is Spring Boot's own integration module, versioned to match Spring Boot's own release (4.1.0) - it is not the Netty library itself. The real Netty here is 4.2.15.Final (see the io.netty:netty-* artifacts in the dependency tree). The CPE matcher collides on the coincidental version-number match with the real, old netty:netty:4.1.0 release from ~2016.

False positive: spring-kafka's own version number (4.1.0) coincidentally matches the CPE for the real, old Spring Framework 4.1.0 (2014). The actual Spring Framework in this project is spring-core 7.0.8, unaffected by CVE-2016-5007.

False positive: CVE-2018-1258 was fixed in Spring Security 4.2.4 / 5.0.3 (2018). All Spring Security modules here (spring-security-core, spring-security-web, ...) share the same 7.1.0 version, far newer than the vulnerable range - the NVD CPE match for this old CVE does not correctly bound the affected version range against modern Spring Security releases. No filePath restriction on purpose - applies to any module in this family, same reasoning as the Kotlin suppression below.

Out of scope: CVE-2026-53914 is unsafe deserialization in the Kotlin compiler's build cache metadata (CWE-502) - a build-time risk for projects that invoke the Kotlin compiler/Gradle build cache. This project is pure Java/Maven and never runs the Kotlin compiler or its build cache; every org.jetbrains.kotlin artifact here (kotlin-stdlib*, kotlin-reflect, kotlin-stdlib-common, ...) is only a transitive runtime jar pulled in via okhttp -> okio (used by the Anthropic Java SDK) or similar. No filePath restriction here on purpose - this CVE doesn't apply to any runtime Kotlin jar regardless of which one it gets matched against. (Also: 2.4.20 is not yet published as a stable Maven Central release, only a beta, so pinning to it isn't currently possible either.)
